OWNER_APPROVAL_v6: EXPLICIT GO on the v6 QAT --run-train, given by the owner in the cloud session 2026-07-01 ('Yes to all' to the v6 GO recommendation). This is the human approvedBy the --run-train gate requires. Authorization scope: ONE v6 QAT training run + its cert. NOT a standing blanket for future --run-train.

v6_blocked_needs_your_deploy.why: The v6 code path is NOT on the spark-bridge checkout the idle poller runs from. Dispatching now would train a v5-IDENTICAL adapter and waste hours. Cloud CANNOT safely deploy it: spark-bridge's tools/train_lora.py has Spark-specific hardening the work-branch lacks (load_kwargs['device_map']={'':0} full-GPU pinning to avoid 'Tensor on device meta is not on the expected device cuda:0'; a _m.training guard in qat.py; a finalTrainLoss NaN-guard). A cloud file-clobber would REGRESS these and crash the train. You must MERGE, not replace.
deploySteps: 1) MERGE the v6 additions into spark-bridge, PRESERVING the bridge hardening: training/qat.py needs kd_top1_margin_loss + qat_bypass + the numpy refs (from work-branch claude/sophia-positioning-gaps-84kb0v HEAD) BUT keep the existing `if not _m.training:` guard; tools/train_lora.py needs the --qat-kd-weight/--qat-top1-weight/--qat-temp/--qat-margin flags + the v6 loss in the train loop (with torch.no_grad(), _qat_bypass(model): fp_logits=...; v6=_kd_top1(...)) BUT keep the device_map={'':0} pinning + trust_remote_code + finalTrainLoss NaN-guard. git cherry-pick/merge from the work branch is the reliable path; do NOT clobber.

2) run_local_benchmarks.sh B1: add to the train_lora invocation, with v5-SAFE defaults so absent env = v5-identical: --qat-kd-weight "${QAT_KD_WEIGHT:-0}" --qat-top1-weight "${QAT_TOP1_WEIGHT:-0}" --qat-temp "${QAT_TEMP:-2.0}" --qat-margin "${QAT_MARGIN:-0}". Add the matching QAT_KD_WEIGHT/QAT_TOP1_WEIGHT/QAT_TEMP/QAT_MARGIN default lines near the other QAT_* defaults.

3) tools/github_bridge_poll.py ENV_ALLOWLIST (and tools/spark_bridge.ENV_ALLOWLIST if present): add QAT_KD_WEIGHT, QAT_TOP1_WEIGHT, QAT_TEMP, QAT_MARGIN (currently only QAT_EPOCHS/QAT_LAMBDA). Without this the poller drops them.

4) VERIFY before GPU: python training/qat.py (expect 15/15 incl the 4 v6 checks); scripts/run_local_benchmarks.sh --bench-b --run-train (DRY-RUN, no --execute) and confirm the printed B1 train_lora command now includes the 4 v6 flags. Only then dispatch with --execute.

v6_run_command.approvedBy: owner explicit GO 2026-07-01 (recorded above) — single v6 run + cert
v6_run_command.note: CERT_NEVAL=1024 so the v6 cert + abstention frontier are on the same firm sample as the v5 confirmation (apples-to-apples). Pre-registered forecast (Spark-Theory-Test-Forecast.md T7): top1 0.95-0.98, GO ~45%. If v6 also misses raw 0.97, the pre-registered next step is depth-mixed precision, NOT another knob — and v5+abstention still ships meanwhile.

v6_blocked_needs_your_deploy.readAfter: Write the v6 cert + frontier (raw top1, mean_kl, shippable_operating_point at confidence=0.95 via serving/abstention_serve) to handoff/from-mac/STATUS.json + a ledger row. Do NOT flip any recipe candidate->adopted; canClaimAGI stays false.

context_v5_shippable: Reminder: v5+abstention already SHIPS at the 95%-robust operating point coverage 0.6426 / answered 0.9970 / floor 0.9831 (n=1024). v6's job is to lift raw coverage so the hedge abstains on far fewer than 36% of tokens. v5 ships regardless of the v6 outcome.
alsoOpen_realModelAblation: Still the other high-value Mac track (A: replication_pack on 70B + verifiability_model.decide; B: counterfactual_traps intervention-consistency; C: novel-knowability generalization). Sequence v6-train and the ablations however your GPU schedule prefers; v6 is the LONG job, the ablations are cheaper.
guardrails: canClaimAGI=false; v6 --run-train authorized ONCE by owner (recorded); merge don't clobber (preserve Spark device_map hardening); dry-run to confirm the v6 flags before --execute; lint_claims + lint_recipe before any receipt commit; do NOT flip candidate->adopted without a real-model ablation + owner sign-off; code only to claude/sophia-positioning-gaps-84kb0v (work) and spark-bridge (bridge deploys); no plaintext secrets.
